what_it_does: Presents a research-wave finding that maps external prior art and the internal design frame for a live, guided, right-hand-man review surface, splitting the problem into a commodity mechanism layer and an unsolved intelligence layer, and argues the make-or-break is grounded comprehension-at-altitude.
observations: The mechanism layer (spotlight, next/back, dwell, tooltip attach, SPA survival) is treated as fully solved by four OSS tour libraries: Driver.js, Intro.js, Shepherd.js, Reactour.
The intelligence layer (live AI sequence composition, altitude narration, cross-time deixis, markup-as-you-talk, generate-to-dispatch) is described as unsolved end-to-end by any shipping product.
Microsoft Copilot Vision is named as the strongest confirmed precedent for the voice + visual-highlight guidance loop.
Figma's AI design review is characterized as async batch critique, not a live narrated walkthrough; AnnotateAI is voice-to-annotation capture, not conversation.
Deixis is framed as a decades-old research problem (Bolt 1980, Tableau 2026 proximity semantics) that remains research-stage, not productized.

The convergence crux is comprehension-at-altitude: external tools lack registered intent/affordance, while internal `address_help` only explains registered elements, leading to confabulation for redesign mockups.
The internal frame asserts the surface was designed as the general human-interaction organ (RHM), with build-review as merely its first consumer.

The honest internal status is described as designed-general, built-partial, live-join-unproven, citing an RHM Deep Scan that says the live conversational seam is unverified, most modes are inert, and `coa` may be unwired.
The file explicitly contradicts anchor naivety on live talk-back and altitude explanation, while defending the anchor's dead-simple consent model.
Evidence tags are used throughout: Observed, Inferred, External-prior-art (URL), External-prior-art (marketing-claim), and Anchor-idea.
standouts: The central split between commodity mechanism and unsolved intelligence.
The claim that comprehension-at-altitude / registered intent is the make-or-break, not the sequencer or consent model.
Microsoft Copilot Vision as proof the voice+highlight loop is buildable, but with a hard limit on registered intent.
The internal general-organ thesis and the counter-finding that the live join is unproven, mirroring the external gap.
The deliberate contradiction of optimistic anchor assumptions while preserving the simple consent model.
conventions: Uses consistent evidence tags and distinguishes confirmed URLs from marketing claims.
Leads with a TL;DR and follows with detailed sections plus a sources list.
Uses comparison tables to organize prior-art layers.
Quotes internal and external sources inline with section/line references.
Maintains a neutral, self-aware tone and actively seeks contradictions rather than cheerleading.
Avoids prescribing the build beyond the file's research scope.
concerns: Some cited external works carry future dates (e.g., 2026) that cannot be independently verified from this file alone.
The Bolt 1980 title is admitted to come from prior knowledge, not the cited snippet.
Heavy reliance on internal references (ANCHOR §X, Observed file:line) that are not resolvable without other files.
The synthesis is long and dense, which may obscure the actionable build priority.
notes: A sober research synthesis arguing the guided review surface's real risk is grounded intent and altitude narration, not tour mechanics, and that the internal general-organ design is ahead of the live-join implementation.
questions: What exact license terms apply to each named OSS tour library?
How is 'altitude' formally defined, measured, or encoded?
Is `coa` truly unwired, or is there a frontend caller not traced in this file?
What are the specific five net-new binds in `Review.tsx` and their current implementation status?
How is the 'dead-simple' consent model implemented in code?
What is the full text of the ANCHOR.md sections referenced throughout?
intention_signals: Reframe the build priority away from tour-engine and consent-model debates toward the harder, unproven comprehension/grounding problem, while validating the surface's general architectural thesis.
novelty: Explicitly fuses external prior-art gaps with internal implementation gaps to identify a single convergence crux (comprehension-at-altitude), and uses that to contradict optimistic anchor assumptions.
contribution: A structured prior-art map separating commodity mechanism from unsolved intelligence, a clear statement that the live join and altitude narration are the riskiest loci, and an honest internal-status check that prevents premature scoping or overclaiming.
summary_for_embedding: A research finding that external tour mechanisms are commodity but live AI-guided review intelligence is unsolved, converging on an internal gap around registered-intent/altitude narration for the Company's general right-hand-man review surface.
intention_for_embedding: To ground the guided review surface's build plan in external reality and internal build status, redirecting risk focus to live comprehension-at-altitude.
